questions.question: Explain the difference between the signal() and sigaction() functions.
expectedPoints: signal() is the older, simpler interface
sigaction() provides more control and information
sigaction() allows specifying additional signals to block during handler execution
sigaction() supports flags like SA_RESTART and SA_SIGINFO

questions.question: Describe the concept of async-signal-safe functions and why they are important in signal handling. Provide examples of safe and unsafe functions.
expectedPoints: Definition: Functions that can be safely called from signal handlers
Importance: Prevents race conditions and undefined behavior
Reasons: Buffered I/O, non-reentrancy, global state modification
Best practices for signal handler design
